data.content: Good, MCP SDK v1.2.0 installed. Let me check the SDK's API surface so I wire things correctly.

arguments.name: explore-mcp-sdk
arguments.prompt:
Explore the ModelContextProtocol NuGet package installed in /tmp/sv-2b74ebe50c7642be883eeef2bc06be4b to understand its API.

1. Find the restored package folder under ~/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol (look for the latest version). List the DLLs.
2. Use `dotnet tool list -g` and check if there's an ilspy or similar. Otherwise use `strings` or similar on the DLL to find public type names.
3. Specifically, I need to know:
   - How to register an MCP server with stdio transport (look for `WithStdioServerTransport`, `AddMcpServer`, `UseMcp`, or similar extension methods)
   - How tools are defined (attributes like `[McpServerToolType]`, `[McpServerTool]`, or interfaces like `ITool`)
   - How prompts are defined (attributes like `[McpServerPromptType]`, `[McpServerPrompt]`, or interfaces)
   - How DI / IServiceProvider works with the server (HostBuilder? WebApplicationBuilder? custom?)
   - What namespaces to use
4. Also check if there's a separate transport package needed, or if stdio is built into the main package.

Be thorough — dump all public type names you can find from the assemblies. Use `dotnet script` or reflection if available, or parse the XML doc files, or use strings/grep on the DLLs.

12.         <member name="M:System.IdHelpers.CreateMonotonicId(System.DateTimeOffset)">
13.             <summary>
14.             Creates a strictly monotonically increasing identifier string using 64-bit timestamp ticks
15.             and a 64-bit counter, formatted as a 32-character hexadecimal string (GUID-like).
16.             </summary>
17.             <param name="timestamp">The timestamp to embed in the identifier.</param>
18.             <returns>A new strictly monotonically increasing identifier string.</returns>
19.             <remarks>
20.             <para>
21.             This method creates a 128-bit identifier composed of two 64-bit values:
22.             - High 64 bits: <see cref="P:System.DateTimeOffset.Ticks"/> from the timestamp
23.             - Low 64 bits: A globally monotonically increasing counter
24.             </para>
25.             <para>
26.             The resulting string is strictly monotonically increasing when compared lexicographically,
27.             which is required for keyset pagination to work correctly. Unlike <c>Guid.CreateVersion7</c>,
28.             which uses random bits for intra-millisecond uniqueness, this implementation guarantees
29.             strict ordering for all identifiers regardless of when they were created.
30.             </para>
31.             </remarks>

346.             </remarks>

357.             <remarks>
358.             <para>
359.             This method scans the specified assembly (or the calling assembly if none is provided) for classes
360.             marked with the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptTypeAttribute"/>. It then discovers all methods within those
361.             classes that are marked with the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute"/> and registers them as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/>s
362.             in the <paramref name="builder"/>'s <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection"/>.
363.             </para>
364.             <para>
365.             The method automatically handles both static and instance methods. For instance methods, a new instance
366.             of the containing class is constructed for each invocation of the prompt.

375.             </para>

457.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithListResourceTemplatesHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListResourceTemplatesRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListResourceTemplatesResult})">
458.             <summary>
459.             Configures a handler for listing resource templates available from the Model Context Protocol server.
460.             </summary>
461.             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
462.             <param name="handler">The handler function that processes resource template list requests.</param>
463.             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
464.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
465.             <remarks>
466.             <para>
467.             This handler is responsible for providing clients with information about available resource templates
468.             that can be used to construct resource URIs.
469.             </para>
470.             <para>
471.             Resource templates describe the structure of resource URIs that the server can handle. They include
472.             URI templates (according to RFC 6570) that clients can use to construct valid resource URIs.
473.             </para>
474.             <para>
475.             This handler is typically paired with <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithReadResourceHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceResult})"/> to provide a complete
476.             resource system where templates define the URI patterns and the read handler provides the actual content.
477.             </para>
478.             </remarks>
479.         </member>
480.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithListToolsHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsResult})">
481.             <summary>
482.             Configures a handler for listing tools available from the Model Context Protocol server.
483.             </summary>
484.             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
485.             <param name="handler">The handler that processes list tools requests.</param>
486.             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
487.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
488.             <remarks>
489.             <para>
490.             This handler is called when a client requests a list of available tools. It should return all tools
491.             that can be invoked through the server, including their names, descriptions, and parameter specifications.
492.             The handler can optionally support pagination via the cursor mechanism for large or dynamically generated
493.             tool collections.
494.             </para>
495.             <para>
496.             When tools are also defined using <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> collection, both sets of tools
497.             will be combined in the response to clients. This allows for a mix of programmatically defined
498.             tools and dynamically generated tools.
499.             </para>
500.             <para>
501.             This method is typically paired with <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithCallToolHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult})"/> to provide a complete tools implementation,
502.             where <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithListToolsHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsResult})"/> advertises available tools and <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithCallToolHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult})"/>
503.             executes them when invoked by clients.
504.             </para>
505.             </remarks>
506.         </member>
507.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithCallToolHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult})">
508.             <summary>
509.             Configures a handler for calling tools available from the Model Context Protocol server.
510.             </summary>
511.             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
512.             <param name="handler">The handler function that processes tool calls.</param>
513.             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
514.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
515.             <remarks>
516.             The call tool handler is responsible for executing custom tools and returning their results to clients.
517.             This method is typically paired with <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithListToolsHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsResult})"/> to provide a complete tools implementation,
518.             where <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithListToolsHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsResult})"/> advertises available tools and this handler executes them.
519.             </remarks>
520.         </member>
521.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithListPromptsHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListPromptsRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListPromptsResult})">

This method is typically paired with <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithGetPromptHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptResult})"/> to provide a complete prompts implementation,

588.             </summary>

596.             </remarks>
597.         </member>
598.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithSubscribeToResourcesHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SubscribeRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmptyResult})">
599.             <summary>
600.             Configures a handler for resource subscription requests.
601.             </summary>
602.             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
603.             <param name="handler">The handler function that processes resource subscription requests.</param>
604.             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
605.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
606.             <remarks>
607.             <para>
608.             The subscribe handler is responsible for registering client interest in specific resources. When a resource
609.             changes, the server can notify all subscribed clients about the change.
610.             </para>
611.             <para>
612.             This handler is typically paired with <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithUnsubscribeFromResourcesHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.UnsubscribeRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmptyResult})"/> to provide a complete
613.             subscription management system. Resource subscriptions allow clients to maintain up-to-date information without
614.             needing to poll resources constantly.
615.             </para>
616.             <para>
617.             After registering a subscription, it's the server's responsibility to track which client is subscribed to which
618.             resources and to send appropriate notifications through the connection when resources change.
619.             </para>
620.             </remarks>
621.         </member>
622.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithUnsubscribeFromResourcesHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.UnsubscribeRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmptyResult})">
623.             <summary>
624.             Configures a handler for resource unsubscription requests.
625.             </summary>
626.             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
627.             <param name="handler">The handler function that processes resource unsubscription requests.</param>
628.             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
629.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
630.             <remarks>
631.             <para>
632.             The unsubscribe handler is responsible for removing client interest in specific resources. When a client
633.             no longer needs to receive notifications about resource changes, it can send an unsubscribe request.
634.             </para>
635.             <para>
636.             This handler is typically paired with <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithSubscribeToResourcesHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SubscribeRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmptyResult})"/> to provide a complete
637.             subscription management system. The unsubscribe operation is idempotent, meaning it can be called multiple
638.             times for the same resource without causing errors, even if there is no active subscription.
639.             </para>
640.             <para>
641.             After removing a subscription, the server should stop sending notifications to the client about changes
642.             to the specified resource.
643.             </para>
644.             </remarks>
645.         </member>
646.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithSetLoggingLevelHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SetLevelRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmptyResult})">
647.             <summary>
648.             Configures a handler for processing logging level change requests from clients.
649.             </summary>
650.             <param name="builder">The server builder instance.</param>
651.             <param name="handler">The handler that processes requests to change the logging level.</param>
652.             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
653.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
654.             <remarks>
655.             <para>
656.             When a client sends a <c>logging/setLevel</c> request, this handler will be invoked to process
657.             the requested level change. The server typically adjusts its internal logging level threshold
658.             and might begin sending log messages at or above the specified level to the client.
659.             </para>
660.             <para>
661.             Regardless of whether a handler is provided, an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> should itself handle
662.             such notifications by updating its <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.LoggingLevel"/> property to return the
663.             most recently set level.
664.             </para>
665.             </remarks>
666.         </member>

 <doc>

     <members>
         <member name="T:System.IdHelpers">
             <summary>
             Provides helper methods for monotonic ID generation.
             </summary>
         </member>
         <member name="M:System.IdHelpers.CreateMonotonicId(System.DateTimeOffset)">
             <summary>
             Creates a strictly monotonically increasing identifier string using 64-bit timestamp ticks
             and a 64-bit counter, formatted as a 32-character hexadecimal string (GUID-like).
             </summary>
             <param name="timestamp">The timestamp to embed in the identifier.</param>
             <returns>A new strictly monotonically increasing identifier string.</returns>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             This method creates a 128-bit identifier composed of two 64-bit values:
             - High 64 bits: <see cref="P:System.DateTimeOffset.Ticks"/> from the timestamp
             - Low 64 bits: A globally monotonically increasing counter
             </para>
             <para>
             The resulting string is strictly monotonically increasing when compared lexicographically,
             which is required for keyset pagination to work correctly. Unlike <c>Guid.CreateVersion7</c>,
             which uses random bits for intra-millisecond uniqueness, this implementation guarantees
             strict ordering for all identifiers regardless of when they were created.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>

             <summary>

             of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) server. This builder is returned by the
             <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions.AddMcpServer(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Action{ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions})"/> extension method and

             in the <paramref name="builder"/>'s <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection"/>.
             </para>
             <para>

             of the containing class is constructed for each invocation of the tool.

         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithListResourceTemplatesHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListResourceTemplatesRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListResourceTemplatesResult})">
             <summary>
             Configures a handler for listing resource templates available from the Model Context Protocol server.
             </summary>
             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
             <param name="handler">The handler function that processes resource template list requests.</param>
             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             This handler is responsible for providing clients with information about available resource templates
             that can be used to construct resource URIs.
             </para>
             <para>
             Resource templates describe the structure of resource URIs that the server can handle. They include
             URI templates (according to RFC 6570) that clients can use to construct valid resource URIs.
             </para>
             <para>
             This handler is typically paired with <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithReadResourceHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceResult})"/> to provide a complete
             resource system where templates define the URI patterns and the read handler provides the actual content.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithListToolsHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListToolsResult})">
             <summary>
             Configures a handler for listing tools available from the Model Context Protocol server.
             </summary>
             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
             <param name="handler">The handler that processes list tools requests.</param>
             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             This handler is called when a client requests a list of available tools. It should return all tools
             that can be invoked through the server, including their names, descriptions, and parameter specifications.
             The handler can optionally support pagination via the cursor mechanism for large or dynamically generated
             tool collections.
             </para>
             <para>

         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithListPromptsHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListPromptsRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListPromptsResult})">

             that can be invoked through the server, including their names, descriptions, and parameter specifications.
             The handler can optionally support pagination via the cursor mechanism for large or dynamically-generated
             prompt collections.

             will be combined in the response to clients. This allows for a mix of programmatically defined
             prompts and dynamically generated prompts.

             <para>

         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithCompleteHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteResult})">
             <summary>
             Configures a handler for auto-completion suggestions for prompt arguments or resource references available from the Model Context Protocol server.
             </summary>
             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
             <param name="handler">The handler function that processes completion requests.</param>

             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>

         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithSubscribeToResourcesHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SubscribeRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmptyResult})">
             <summary>
             Configures a handler for resource subscription requests.
             </summary>
             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
             <param name="handler">The handler function that processes resource subscription requests.</param>
             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             The subscribe handler is responsible for registering client interest in specific resources. When a resource
             changes, the server can notify all subscribed clients about the change.
             </para>
             <para>
             This handler is typically paired with <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithUnsubscribeFromResourcesHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.UnsubscribeRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmptyResult})"/> to provide a complete
             subscription management system. Resource subscriptions allow clients to maintain up-to-date information without
             needing to poll resources constantly.
             </para>
             <para>
             After registering a subscription, it's the server's responsibility to track which client is subscribed to which
             resources and to send appropriate notifications through the connection when resources change.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithUnsubscribeFromResourcesHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.UnsubscribeRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmptyResult})">
             <summary>
             Configures a handler for resource unsubscription requests.
             </summary>
             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
             <param name="handler">The handler function that processes resource unsubscription requests.</param>
             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             The unsubscribe handler is responsible for removing client interest in specific resources. When a client
             no longer needs to receive notifications about resource changes, it can send an unsubscribe request.
             </para>
             <para>
             This handler is typically paired with <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithSubscribeToResourcesHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SubscribeRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmptyResult})"/> to provide a complete
             subscription management system. The unsubscribe operation is idempotent, meaning it can be called multiple
             times for the same resource without causing errors, even if there is no active subscription.
             </para>
             <para>
             After removing a subscription, the server should stop sending notifications to the client about changes
             to the specified resource.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithSetLoggingLevelHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SetLevelRequestParams,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmptyResult})">
             <summary>
             Configures a handler for processing logging level change requests from clients.
             </summary>
             <param name="builder">The server builder instance.</param>
             <param name="handler">The handler that processes requests to change the logging level.</param>
             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             When a client sends a <c>logging/setLevel</c> request, this handler will be invoked to process
             the requested level change. The server typically adjusts its internal logging level threshold
             and might begin sending log messages at or above the specified level to the client.
             </para>
             <para>
             Regardless of whether a handler is provided, an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> should itself handle
             such notifications by updating its <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.LoggingLevel"/> property to return the
             most recently set level.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>

         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithStdioServerTransport(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IMcpServerBuilder)">
             <summary>
             Adds a server transport that uses standard input (stdin) and standard output (stdout) for communication.
             </summary>
             <param name="builder">The builder instance.</param>
             <returns>The builder provided in <paramref name="builder"/>.</returns>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="builder"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             This method configures the server to communicate using the standard input and output streams,
             which is commonly used when the Model Context Protocol server is launched locally by a client process.
             </para>
             <para>
             When using this transport, the server runs as a single-session service that exits when the
             stdin stream is closed. This makes it suitable for scenarios where the server should terminate
             when the parent process disconnects.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>

         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStore">
             <summary>
             An <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.ISseEventStreamStore"/> implementation backed by <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Distributed.IDistributedCache"/>.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             This implementation stores SSE events in a distributed cache, enabling resumability across
             multiple server instances. Event IDs are encoded with session, stream, and sequence information
             to allow efficient retrieval of events after a given point.
             </para>
             <para>
             The writer maintains in-memory state for sequence number generation, as there is guaranteed
             to be only one writer per stream. Readers may be created from separate processes.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>

             Gets or sets the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Distributed.IDistributedCache"/> to use for event storage.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             When using dependency injection with <c>WithDistributedCacheEventStreamStore()</c>, this is
             automatically populated from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.Caching.Distributed.IDistributedCache"/> registered in DI.
             Set this property explicitly to use a specific cache instance.
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.EventSlidingExpiration">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets the sliding expiration for individual events in the cache.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             Events are refreshed on each access. If an event is not accessed within this
             time period, it may be evicted from the cache.
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.EventAbsoluteExpiration">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets the absolute expiration for individual events in the cache.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             Events will be evicted from the cache after this time period, regardless of access.
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.MetadataSlidingExpiration">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets the sliding expiration for stream metadata in the cache.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             Stream metadata includes mode and completion status. This should typically be
             set to a longer duration than event expiration to allow for resumability.
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.MetadataAbsoluteExpiration">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets the absolute expiration for stream metadata in the cache.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             Stream metadata will be evicted from the cache after this time period, regardless of access.
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.DistributedCacheEventStreamStoreOptions.StreamReaderPollingInterval">
             <summary>
             Gets or sets the interval between polling attempts when a stream reader is waiting for new events
             in the default <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Server.SseEventStreamMode.Streaming"/> mode.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             This only affects stream readers. A shorter interval provides lower latency for new events
             but increases cache access frequency.
             </remarks>
         </member>
         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.SingleSessionMcpServerHostedService">
             <summary>
             Hosted service for a single-session (e.g. stdio) MCP server.
             </summary>
             <param name="session">The server representing the session being hosted.</param>
             <param name="lifetime">
             The host's application lifetime. If available, it will have termination requested when the session's run completes.
             </param>
         </member>
         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.SingleSessionMcpServerHostedService.#ctor(ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer,Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.IHostApplicationLifetime)">

             </param>
         </member>
         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.SingleSessionMcpServerHostedService.ExecuteAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
             <inheritdoc />
         </member>
         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Throw">
             <summary>Provides helper methods for throwing exceptions.</summary>
         </member>
         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Obsoletions">
             <summary>
             Defines diagnostic IDs, messages, and URLs for APIs annotated with <see cref="T:System.ObsoleteAttribute"/>.
             </summary>
             <remarks>
             When a deprecated API is associated with a specification change, the message
             should refer to the specification version that introduces the change and the SEP
             when available. If there is a SEP associated with the experimental API, the Url should
             point to the SEP issue.
             <para>
             Obsolete diagnostic IDs are in the format MCP9###.
             </para>
             <para>
             Diagnostic IDs cannot be reused when obsolete APIs are removed or restored.
             This ensures that users do not suppress warnings for new diagnostics with existing
             suppressions that might be left in place from prior uses of the same diagnostic ID.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>
     </members>

result.content: Gets or sets the function that's invoked when resource metadata is requested. This function is called when a resource metadata request is made to the protected resource metadata endpoint. The implementer should set the property to provide the appropriate metadata for the current request. Represents an authentication handler for MCP protocol that adds resource metadata to challenge responses and handles resource metadata endpoint requests. Initializes a new instance of the class. Gets the absolute URI for the resource metadata endpoint. Represents options for the MCP authentication handler. Initializes a new instance of the class. Gets or sets the events used to handle authentication events. Gets or sets the URI to the resource metadata document. This URI is included in the WWW-Authenticate header when a 401 response is returned. When , the handler automatically uses the default /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource/<resource-path> endpoint that mirrors the requested resource path. Gets or sets the protected resource metadata. This contains the OAuth metadata for the protected resource, including authorization servers, supported scopes, and other information needed for clients to authenticate. Represents the context for resource metadata request events. Initializes a new instance of the class. The HTTP context. The authentication scheme. The authentication options. Gets or sets the protected resource metadata for the current request. Evaluates authorization policies from endpoint metadata. Evaluates authorization policies from endpoint metadata. Filters a collection of items based on authorization policies in their metadata. For list operations where we need to filter results by authorization. Combines authorization policies and requirements from endpoint metadata without considering . The authorization policy provider. The endpoint metadata collection. The combined authorization policy, or null if no authorization is required. Configures by resolving the from DI when not explicitly set. Configures by resolving the from DI when not explicitly set. Validates that is set. Represents configuration options for , which implements the Streamable HTTP transport for the Model Context Protocol. See the protocol specification for details on the Streamable HTTP transport. For details on the Streamable HTTP transport, see the protocol specification. Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback to configure per-session with access to the of the request that initiated the session. In stateful mode (the default), this callback is invoked once per session when the client sends the initialize request. In mode, it is invoked on every HTTP request because each request creates a fresh server context. Gets or sets an optional asynchronous callback for running new MCP sessions manually. This callback is useful for running logic before a session starts and after it completes. The parameter comes from the request that initiated the session (e.g., the initialize request) and may not be usable after starts, since that request will have already completed. Consider using instead, which provides access to the of the initializing request with fewer known issues. This API is experimental and may be removed or change signatures in a future release. Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the server runs in a stateless mode that doesn't track state between requests, allowing for load balancing without session affinity. if the server runs in a stateless mode; if the server tracks state between requests. The default is . If , will be null, and the "MCP-Session-Id" header will not be used, the will be called once for each request, and the "/sse" endpoint will be disabled. Unsolicited server-to-client messages and all server-to-client requests are also unsupported, because any responses might arrive at another ASP.NET Core application process. Client sampling, elicitation, and roots capabilities are also disabled in stateless mode, because the server cannot make requests. Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the server maps legacy SSE endpoints (/sse and /message) for backward compatibility with clients that do not support the Streamable HTTP transport. to map the legacy SSE endpoints; to disable them. The default is . The legacy SSE transport separates request and response channels: clients POST JSON-RPC messages to /message and receive responses through a long-lived GET SSE stream on /sse. Because the POST endpoint returns 202 Accepted immediately, there is no HTTP-level backpressure on handler concurrency — unlike Streamable HTTP, where each POST is held open until the handler responds. Use Streamable HTTP instead whenever possible. If you must support legacy SSE clients, enable this property only for completely trusted clients in isolated processes, and apply HTTP rate-limiting middleware and reverse proxy limits to compensate for the lack of built-in backpressure. Setting this to while is also throws an at startup, because SSE requires in-memory session state. This property can also be enabled via the ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore.EnableLegacySse switch. Gets or sets the event store for resumability support. When set, events are stored and can be replayed when clients reconnect with a Last-Event-ID header. When configured, the server will: Generate unique event IDs for each SSE message Store events for later replay Replay missed events when a client reconnects with a Last-Event-ID header Send priming events to establish resumability before any actual messages This can be set directly, or an can be registered in DI. If this property is not set, the server will attempt to resolve an from DI. Gets or sets the session migration handler for cross-instance session migration. When configured, the server will support session migration between instances. If a request arrives with a session ID that is not found locally, the handler is consulted to determine if the session can be migrated from another instance. This can be set directly, or an can be registered in DI.

1. HOW TO REGISTER MCP SERVER WITH STDIO TRANSPORT?
   ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   
   Namespace: Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection
   
   Extension Method:
   ├─ AddMcpServer(this IServiceCollection services, Action<McpServerOptions> configureOptions)
   │  Returns: IMcpServerBuilder
   │  Location: McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions
   │
   └─ Then call: WithStdioServerTransport(this IMcpServerBuilder builder)
      Returns: IMcpServerBuilder
      Location: McpServerBuilderExtensions

   Typical Usage:
   ──────────────
   var services = new ServiceCollection();
   services.AddMcpServer(options => { /* configure */ })
           .WithStdioServerTransport();
   
   Alternative Stream Transport:
   ──────────────────────────────
   .WithStreamServerTransport(inputStream, outputStream)

================================================================================

2. HOW TO DEFINE TOOLS?
   ────────────────────

   METHOD 1: Using Attributes (Most Common)
   ─────────────────────────────────────────
   
   Class-Level Attribute:
   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   │ [McpServerToolType]
   │ public class MyTools
   │ {
   │     [McpServerTool]
   │     public string GetWeather(string location)
   │     {
   │         return "Sunny in " + location;
   │     }
   │ }
   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   
   Attributes:
   ├─ McpServerToolTypeAttribute (on class)
   │  - Marks the class as containing tool definitions
   │  - Scanned by WithToolsFromAssembly()
   │
   └─ McpServerToolAttribute (on method)
     - Marks a method as a tool handler
     - Supports both instance and static methods
     - Parameters are automatically marshalled from JSON

   Registration:
   ───────────────
   builder.WithTools<MyTools>(jsonSerializerOptions)
      OR
   builder.WithToolsFromAssembly(Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly(), jsonSerializerOptions)

   METHOD 2: Using Direct Type Registration
   ─────────────────────────────────────────
   builder.WithTools<MyTools>(targetInstance, jsonSerializerOptions)
   builder.WithTools(new[] { typeof(MyTools) }, jsonSerializerOptions)

   METHOD 3: Direct McpServerTool Registration
   ────────────────────────────────────────────
   builder.WithTools(enumerable<McpServerTool>)

   Key Types:
   ──────────
   ├─ McpServerTool (represents a tool definition)
   ├─ McpServerToolAttribute (method-level attribute)
   ├─ McpServerToolTypeAttribute (class-level attribute)
   └─ McpServerToolCreateOptions (configuration for tool creation)

================================================================================

3. HOW TO DEFINE PROMPTS?
   ──────────────────────

   Identical Pattern to Tools:
   
   Class-Level Attribute:
   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   │ [McpServerPromptType]
   │ public class MyPrompts
   │ {
   │     [McpServerPrompt]
   │     public IEnumerable<ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage>
   │     GetSystemPrompt(string context)
   │     {
   │         yield return new PromptMessage { ... };
   │     }
   │ }
   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

   Attributes:
   ├─ McpServerPromptTypeAttribute (on class)
   │  - Marks the class as containing prompt definitions
   │  - Scanned by WithPromptsFromAssembly()
   │
   └─ McpServerPromptAttribute (on method)
     - Marks a method as a prompt handler
     - Returns IEnumerable<PromptMessage>

   Registration:
   ───────────────
   builder.WithPrompts<MyPrompts>(jsonSerializerOptions)
      OR
   builder.WithPromptsFromAssembly(Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly(), jsonSerializerOptions)

   Key Types:
   ──────────
   ├─ McpServerPrompt (represents a prompt definition)
   ├─ McpServerPromptAttribute (method-level attribute)
   ├─ McpServerPromptTypeAttribute (class-level attribute)
   └─ McpServerPromptCreateOptions (configuration for prompt creation)

================================================================================

4. HOW DO RESOURCES WORK?
   ──────────────────────

   Class-Level Attribute:
   ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   │ [McpServerResourceType]
   │ public class MyResources
   │ {
   │     [McpServerResource]
   │     public string GetFile(string path)
   │     {
   │         return File.ReadAllText(path);
   │     }
   │ }
   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

   Attributes:
   ├─ McpServerResourceTypeAttribute (on class)
   │  - Marks the class as containing resource definitions
   │
   └─ McpServerResourceAttribute (on method or property)
     - Marks a member as providing a resource

   Registration:
   ───────────────
   builder.WithResources<MyResources>()
      OR
   builder.WithResourcesFromAssembly(Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly())

   Key Types:
   ──────────
   ├─ McpServerResource (represents a resource definition)
   ├─ McpServerResourceAttribute (member-level attribute)
   ├─ McpServerResourceTypeAttribute (class-level attribute)
   └─ McpServerResourceCreateOptions (configuration for resource creation)

================================================================================

5. HOW DOES DI / SERVICEPROVIDER WORK?
   ───────────────────────────────────

   DI Integration:
   ───────────────
   ├─ AddMcpServer() works with Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection
   │
   ├─ Returns IMcpServerBuilder interface which exposes:
   │  ├─ Services property (access to IServiceCollection)
   │  └─ Builder pattern for fluent configuration
   │
   └─ DefaultMcpServerBuilder is the built-in implementation

   Key Builder Methods:
   ────────────────────
   ├─ WithTools<T>() / WithToolsFromAssembly()
   ├─ WithPrompts<T>() / WithPromptsFromAssembly()
   ├─ WithResources<T>() / WithResourcesFromAssembly()
   ├─ WithListToolsHandler() / WithCallToolHandler()
   ├─ WithListPromptsHandler() / WithGetPromptHandler()
   ├─ WithListResourcesHandler() / WithReadResourceHandler()
   ├─ WithMessageFilters()
   ├─ WithRequestFilters()
   ├─ WithStdioServerTransport()
   └─ WithStreamServerTransport()

   Request Handler Types:
   ──────────────────────
   ├─ McpRequestHandler<TParams, TResult>
   │  - Delegate for custom request handling
   │  - Signature: async Task<TResult> HandleAsync(TParams params, CancellationToken ct)
   │
   ├─ McpMessageHandler
   │  - Delegate for handling JSON-RPC messages
   │
   └─ McpMessageFilter / McpRequestFilter
     - For intercepting and modifying requests/responses

   Server Instance:
   ────────────────
   ├─ Resolved from IServiceProvider as: McpServer
   ├─ Methods:
   │  ├─ RunAsync(CancellationToken) - starts the server session
   │  ├─ SendNotificationAsync(...)  - sends unsolicited notifications
   │  └─ LoggingLevel property       - controls logging
   │
   └─ Hosted Service:
     └─ Use SingleSessionMcpServerHostedService for background hosting

   NO HostBuilder/WebApplicationBuilder integration in main package.
   (AspNetCore package may provide HTTP-specific integration via MapMcp())

================================================================================

   METHOD 1: Using Attributes (Most Common)
   ─────────────────────────────────────────

   │ {

   │     public string GetWeather(string location)
   │     {
   │         return "Sunny in " + location;
   │     }
   │ }
   └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   
   Attributes:
   ├─ McpServerToolTypeAttribute (on class)
   │  - Marks the class as containing tool definitions
   │  - Scanned by WithToolsFromAssembly()
   │
   └─ McpServerToolAttribute (on method)
     - Marks a method as a tool handler
     - Supports both instance and static methods
     - Parameters are automatically marshalled from JSON

   Registration:
   ───────────────
   builder.WithTools<MyTools>(jsonSerializerOptions)
      OR
   builder.WithToolsFromAssembly(Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly(), jsonSerializerOptions)

   METHOD 2: Using Direct Type Registration
   ─────────────────────────────────────────
   builder.WithTools<MyTools>(targetInstance, jsonSerializerOptions)
   builder.WithTools(new[] { typeof(MyTools) }, jsonSerializerOptions)

   ────────────────────────────────────────────
   builder.WithTools(enumerable<McpServerTool>)

data.content:
Perfect! Now let me create a final comprehensive response combining all this information:

## Complete ModelContextProtocol 1.2.0 API Analysis

I've thoroughly explored the ModelContextProtocol NuGet package. Here's the comprehensive breakdown:

### **Package Overview**

**Installed Packages:**
- **ModelContextProtocol** v1.2.0 - Main server/client/protocol package
- **ModelContextProtocol.Core** v1.2.0 (dependency) - Core implementations  
- **ModelContextProtocol.AspNetCore** v1.2.0 (optional) - HTTP transport support

**Frameworks Supported:** net10.0, net9.0, net8.0, netstandard2.0

---

### **1. Stdio Server Registration**

**Extension Method:**
```csharp
Namespace: Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection

services.AddMcpServer(options => { /* configure */ })
        .WithStdioServerTransport();
```

- **`AddMcpServer()`** - Returns `IMcpServerBuilder`
  - Location: `McpServerServiceCollectionExtensions`
  - Integrates with standard DI (`IServiceCollection`)

- **`.WithStdioServerTransport()`** - Chainable
  - Location: `McpServerBuilderExtensions`
  - Handles stdio communication (stdin/stdout)
  - Single-session, terminates when stdin closes

            <summary>
            Converts a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> to a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/> object.
            </summary>

            <param name="options">The <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> to use for deserialization. If <see langword="null"/>, <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.McpJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/> is used.</param>

            <remarks>
            This method transforms a protocol-specific <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> from the Model Context Protocol
            into a standard <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/> object that can be used with AI client libraries.
            </remarks>
            <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="promptMessage"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>

result.content:
<member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage">
            <summary>
            Represents a message within the Model Context Protocol (MCP) system, used for communication between clients and AI models.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            A <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> encapsulates content sent to or received from AI models in the Model Context Protocol.
            Each message has a specific role (<see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.User"/> or <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.Assistant"/>) and contains content, which can be
            text, images, audio, or embedded resources.
            </para>
            <para>
            This class is similar to <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMessage"/>, but with enhanced support for embedding resources from the MCP server.
            It serves as a core data structure in the MCP message exchange flow, particularly in prompt formation and model responses.
            </para>
            <para>
            <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> objects are typically used in collections within <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptResult"/>
            to represent complete conversations or prompt sequences. They can be converted to and from <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/>
            objects using the extension methods <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToChatMessage(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)"/> and
            <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToPromptMessages(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage)"/>.
            </para>
            <para>
            See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage.Content">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the content of the message, which can be text, image, audio, or an embedded resource.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            The <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage.Content"/> object contains all the message payload, whether it's simple text,
            base64-encoded binary data (for images/audio), or a reference to an embedded resource.
            The <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Type"/> property indicates the specific content type.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage.Role">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the role of the message sender, specifying whether it's from a "user" or an "assistant".
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            In the Model Context Protocol, each message must have a clear role assignment to maintain
            the conversation flow. User messages represent queries or inputs from users, while assistant
            messages represent responses generated by AI models.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptsCapability">
            <summary>
            Represents the server's capability to provide predefined prompt templates that clients can use.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            The prompts capability allows a server to expose a collection of predefined prompt templates that clients
            can discover and use. These prompts can be static (defined in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.PromptCollection"/>) or
            dynamically generated through handlers.
            </para>
            <para>
            See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptsCapability.ListChanged">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets a value that indicates whether this server supports notifications for changes to the prompt list.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            When set to <see langword="true"/>, the server will send notifications using
            <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.PromptListChangedNotification"/> when prompts are added,
            removed, or modified. Clients can register handlers for these notifications to
            refresh their prompt cache. This capability enables clients to stay synchronized with server-side changes
            to available prompts.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceRequestParams">
            <summary>
            Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesRead"/> request from a client to get a resource provided by a server.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            The server will respond with a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceResult"/> containing the resulting resource data.
            </para>
            <para>
            Alternatively, if the resource URI uses the <c>https://</c> scheme, clients may fetch the resource
            directly from the web instead of using <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesRead"/>.
            Servers should only use the <c>https://</c> scheme when the client is able to fetch and load the
            resource directly on its own.
            </para>
            <para>
            See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceRequestParams.Uri">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the URI of the resource to read. The URI can use any protocol; it is up to the server how to interpret it.
            </summary>
        </member>
        <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceResult">
            <summary>
            Represents a server's response to a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesRead"/> request from the client.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
<exited with exit code 0>

<member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock">
            <summary>
            Represents content within the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            The <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> class is a fundamental type in the MCP that can represent different forms of content
            based on the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Type"/> property. Derived types like <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock"/>, <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock"/>,
            and <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmbeddedResourceBlock"/> provide the type-specific content.
            </para>
            <para>
            This class is used throughout the MCP for representing content in messages, tool responses,
            and other communication between clients and servers.
            </para>
            <para>
            See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for more details.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.#ctor">
            <summary>Prevent external derivations.</summary>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Type">
            <summary>
            When overridden in a derived class, gets the type of content.
            </summary>
            <value>
            The type of content. Valid values include "image", "audio", "text", "resource", "resource_link", "tool_use", and "tool_result".
            </value>
            <remarks>
            This value determines the structure of the content object.

            <summary>
            Represents a root URI and its metadata in the Model Context Protocol.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            Root URIs serve as entry points for resource navigation, typically representing
            top-level directories or container resources that are relevant to the current session.
            Roots inform servers which locations the client considers important, providing informational
            guidance rather than an access-control mechanism. Each root has a URI that uniquely identifies
            it and optional metadata like a human-readable name.
            </remarks>

import clr
import sys

# Add the DLL to the assembly path
dll_path = "/home/runner/.nuget/packages/modelcontextprotocol.core/1.2.0/lib/net9.0/ModelContextProtocol.Core.dll"

try:
    # Load the assembly
    from System.Reflection import Assembly
    assembly = Assembly.LoadFrom(dll_path)
    
    # Get the types
    prompt_msg_type = assembly.GetType("ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage")
    role_type = assembly.GetType("ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role")
    content_block_type = assembly.GetType("ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock")
    
    if prompt_msg_type:
        print("=== PromptMessage Type ===")
        print(f"Type Name: {prompt_msg_type.FullName}")
        print("\nProperties:")
        for prop in prompt_msg_type.GetProperties():
            print(f"  - {prop.Name}: {prop.PropertyType.Name}")
    
    if role_type:
        print("\n=== Role Type ===")
        print(f"Type Name: {role_type.FullName}")
        print(f"Is Enum: {role_type.IsEnum}")
        if role_type.IsEnum:
            print("Enum Fields:")
            for field in role_type.GetFields():
                if not field.Name.startswith("_"):
                    print(f"  - {field.Name}")
    
    if content_block_type:
        print("\n=== ContentBlock Type ===")
        print(f"Type Name: {content_block_type.FullName}")
        print(f"Is Abstract: {content_block_type.IsAbstract}")
        print("Properties:")
        for prop in content_block_type.GetProperties():
            print(f"  - {prop.Name}: {prop.PropertyType.Name}")

except Exception as e:
    print(f"Error: {e}")
    import traceback
    traceback.print_exc()
EOF

result.content:
<member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage">
            <summary>
            Represents a message within the Model Context Protocol (MCP) system, used for communication between clients and AI models.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            <para>
            A <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> encapsulates content sent to or received from AI models in the Model Context Protocol.
            Each message has a specific role (<see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.User"/> or <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.Assistant"/>) and contains content, which can be
            text, images, audio, or embedded resources.
            </para>
            <para>
            This class is similar to <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMessage"/>, but with enhanced support for embedding resources from the MCP server.
            It serves as a core data structure in the MCP message exchange flow, particularly in prompt formation and model responses.
            </para>
            <para>
            <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> objects are typically used in collections within <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptResult"/>
            to represent complete conversations or prompt sequences. They can be converted to and from <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/>
            objects using the extension methods <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToChatMessage(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)"/> and
            <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToPromptMessages(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage)"/>.
            </para>
            <para>
            See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
            </para>
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage.Content">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the content of the message, which can be text, image, audio, or an embedded resource.
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            The <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage.Content"/> object contains all the message payload, whether it's simple text,
            base64-encoded binary data (for images/audio), or a reference to an embedded resource.
            The <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Type"/> property indicates the specific content type.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage.Role">
            <summary>
            Gets or sets the role of the message sender, specifying whether it's from a "user" or an "assistant".
            </summary>
            <remarks>
            In the Model Context Protocol, each message must have a clear role assignment to maintain
            the conversation flow. User messages represent queries or inputs from users, while assistant
            messages represent responses generated by AI models.
            </remarks>
        </member>
        <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptsCapability">
<exited with exit code 0>
